location.office_time_required: four days/week in-office, with no required office days during the summer and winter holidays

level_signals: Owns product roadmap across identity verification, authentication, and account security
Leads cross-functional delivery from problem definition through launch
Comp range $121K-$143K base in NYC, consistent with mid-to-senior IC PM

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Product management experience building products in fintech, financial services, or other regulated / risk-heavy domains (e.g., payments, security, marketplaces).
Strong analytical and data-informed decision-making skills, comfortable using metrics, experiments, and qualitative inputs (CX tickets, risk case data, user research) to set direction.
Ability to balance fraud/risk reduction with customer experience and growth, and clearly communicate the tradeoffs you're making.
Proven success leading cross-functional work with engineering, design, CX, Operations, Compliance, Legal, and Security to ship complex features.
Comfort with concepts like KYC/AML, identity verification, authentication, ACH and card risk, account takeover, and device intelligence (or a demonstrated ability to ramp quickly).
Excellent written and verbal communication and storytelling skills; able to create clear specs and influence stakeholders at multiple levels.
Strong sense of ownership and a track record of driving work forward in ambiguous environments while maintaining a high bar for quality and customer trust.

scope.surface: User Trust — identity verification, authentication, device risk, behavioral signals, account security for Betterment's retail investing and cash products; KYC/KYB, 2FA, risk scoring, transaction monitoring, case management controls
scope.business_model: B2C and B2B fintech — retail investing, savings, retirement, 401(k) for SMBs
primary_metrics_inferred: Customer experience quality (friction vs. security balance)
core_responsibilities: Own the product roadmap across identity verification, authentication, device risk, behavioral signals, and account security for Betterment's retail investing and cash products
Identify and prioritize opportunities to increase security and integrity while minimizing friction in signup, funding, withdrawals, login, and sensitive profile changes

Partner with Operations, Security Engineering, Customer Experience, and Compliance to design, implement, and iterate on KYC/KYB, 2FA, risk scoring, transaction monitoring, and case management controls
Work closely with engineering to define solutions and tradeoffs, while actively questioning assumptions and default approaches before converging on an implementation
Lead cross-functional delivery with design, engineering, analytics, and operations from problem definition through discovery, specs, launch, and iteration
Run experiments and tests to calibrate trust controls, including where to require additional verification, how to tune thresholds, and how to communicate risk decisions
Define and track success metrics that balance platform integrity with customer experience, including financial risk, login success rates, and conversion
Build deep insight into customers and internal operators through research, risk and incident reviews, CX shadowing, and market and competitive analysis

compensation.equity_form: Equity for all employees, including new hire and refresher grants

green_flags: Compensation range explicitly disclosed ($121K-$143K base in NYC)
Equity offered to all employees including refresher grants
Clear product surface and ownership area (identity, authentication, risk controls)
Explicitly acknowledges candidates who can ramp quickly on domain concepts, reducing hard barrier
Flexible PTO and paid sabbatical at year six signal strong employee investment
Day-one 401(k) match and student loan payment matching are competitive benefits
red_flags: Four days/week in-office requirement is unusually high and eliminates most remote candidates
Role is NYC-based only — limited geographic flexibility
No minimum years of experience stated, making seniority level ambiguous
Visa sponsorship not mentioned; unclear whether international candidates are eligible

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range and scope of ownership)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly listed as metrics in JD)
ambiguities: No minimum years of PM experience are stated; seniority is inferred from compensation and scope of responsibilities
Visa sponsorship is not addressed anywhere in the JD; 'unclear' chosen rather than defaulting to 'no'
Nice-to-haves section is absent from the JD — all requirements are in a single 'What We're Looking For' section with no explicit tiering; all treated as hard based on unconditional language
